gate_history: Round 1 (9 f2p): Sonnet 0/5 (pass), Opus 7/10 (above the internal Opus<=6/10 bar). Hardened to 12 f2p by adding 3 under-constrained permutations: empty-string invite token (both-required guard), payload-segment tamper (signature binds the invite context, distinct from the existing signature-tail tamper), and a callback security test (a state whose invite context fails verification must fall back to the generic redirect, not grant the invite return page). Awaiting Opus re-gate.
visible_suite_green: n/a — loangen-agent/tests/ does not exist at this base; all tests enter via test_patch, so there is no agent-visible test file to keep green
fairness_audit: Public seams only: state round-trip asserted via build_auth_url + get_state_context (both published as import contract), redirects via the qb_callback handler. The only mocked collaborator is handle_oauth_callback (the module's public service function every correct router must call), replaced with an explicit SimpleNamespace(user_id=...) return — no bare MagicMock — while the redirect decision runs the real get_state_context over a real signed state. Alternative-implementation audit: an impl keying off invite_token presence vs the oracle's explicit flow/invite_token keys both satisfy the published get_state_context contract; the redirect path and qb_success/qb_error markers are published, so router tests assert contract not implementation. Settings touched only via patch.object(settings_singleton, 'frontend_url', ...). The 3 hardening tests stay inside the same fair envelope: empty-string invite token and payload tamper are pure build_auth_url + get_state_context round-trips (each derivable from the published 'partial/absent context must not be embedded' and 'modified/malformed token yields no usable context' clauses); the callback security test builds a real invite state, tampers its payload, and asserts the REAL get_state_context (over a real signature check) forces the generic redirect — no new unpublished names, no convention-picks (an impl that verifies the signature and honours the both-required guard passes them all).
litmus_result: Instruction names the QuickBooks OAuth service/router only as the two imported public functions (build_auth_url signature + get_state_context) plus the asserted redirect contract — the same API-contract exception the canonical used. It does not specify the both-required embedding guard, the non-invite-flow exclusion, the tamper/malformed handling, the payload structure, or how the callback wires the context into the success/failure redirect branches; a literal fix that just adds a flow kwarg fails test_invite_flow_requires_a_token and test_only_invite_flow_value_is_carried.
notes: Real April-2026 fix mined from efab779. The repo image carries full future history, so the environment/Dockerfile rebuilds a single-commit git history (rm -rf .git && git init) after resetting to base, hiding the fix commit from git log/show/reflog. 12 f2p span 2 source files: 9 on the OAuth state contract, 3 on the callback redirect (a 2-hop distance defect: the invite token in the signed state surfaces as the redirect URL). Difficulty=medium-hard after hardening: breadth + distance + permutation coverage (empty vs None vs non-invite context; payload vs signature tamper; tamper-does-not-grant-invite) per CONSTRUCTION_V2 rule 5.
